entities.name: Jaymart Group Holdings Public Company Limited

financial.note: KBANK serves as the registrar for major 2026 debenture issuances (e.g., JMART292A); rising interest rates increase refinancing costs for their ~฿8.8bn debt pool.
Lead manager for the ฿2.0bn debenture rollout in April 2026; financial performance is sensitive to the success of capital market fundraising.

supply_chain.note: Collaborates on the 'Samsung Finance+' model; sales volume is highly sensitive to Samsung's flagship launch cycles and credit approval rates.
Provides the 'Locked Phone' loan platform (SG Finance+) which is the primary driver for JMART Mobile's 2026 growth strategy.
Utilizes the BTS Group advertising and distribution network to reach urban commuters; performance is tied to VGI's retail ecosystem.

competitors.note: Primary rival in tech-retail; competition for iPhone/Android market share and consumer financing dominance.
Indirect rival via JMT in the distressed debt acquisition market; share of capital for NPL portfolios.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: As a finance-heavy holding company, rate hikes compress margins for SINGER/SGC and increase JMT's cost of capital for debt purchases.
Increases the supply of NPLs for JMT but serves as a headwind for the retail division's purchasing power.
Weak THB increases the landed cost of imported smartphones, potentially squeezing retail gross margins if price hikes aren't passed on.
